brief_justification: Arrives at exactly $11,166 with each arithmetic step correct; presents a flat computation table with no exploration of why the mechanics work or second-order implications; proceeds linearly without questioning assumptions or naming potential misinterpretations; shows all intermediate values row-by-row but never re-derives from a different angle or applies a sanity check; states the answer with full certainty and does not acknowledge timing assumptions (interest before vs. after transaction); never considers alternative interpretations of the problem (e.g., continuous compounding, different transaction timing); every column in the table is explicitly shown so a third party can reproduce the full chain without any external context.
